plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs to reduce volatility.
Increase allocation to income-generating QQQI for stability.
Reduce exposure in SPY and QQQ to lower concentration.
Add Microsoft (MSFT) as a high-quality tech holding.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
Focus on lower-risk, steady growth with tight stops.
conservative.read: This plan aims for ~1.5% monthly growth with capital preservation as the priority. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, suitable for risk-averse trading.

plan: Reduce exposure in leveraged ETF SOXL and exit SPAL.
Trim SPY to meet concentration mandate, increase QQQ.
Add Microsoft (MSFT) for quality tech exposure.
Initiate a position in NVDU based on strong scanner signal.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
Balance growth with risk reduction, targeting ~2.5% monthly.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% monthly growth by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and select high-potential setups, managing risk carefully.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL.
Significantly reduce broad index exposure (SPY, QQQ).
Add NVDU and MSFT aggressively based on scanner strength.
Increase position in winning CRWD for concentrated growth.
Maintain a small cash buffer, accepting higher risk.
Targeting ~4% monthly growth with higher potential drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by concentrating on high-scoring setups and increasing leveraged ETF exposure. Expect significantly higher potential gains and losses, with daily leverage decay being a key factor.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged and high-beta single name positions.
Maximize allocation to income-focused QQQI.
Increase allocation to SPY for stability and broad exposure.
Reduce exposure in the more volatile QQQ index.
Hold a larger cash buffer to park funds for yield.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing capital preservation and steady yield. It shifts away from growth and leverage towards income-generating assets and stable broad market exposure.
